data.title: MeiraGTx gets FDA RMAT status for its Parkinson’s disease treatment
data.description:
• MeiraGTx (NASDAQ:MGTX) said on Friday that the U.S. FDA has granted regenerative medicine advanced therapy (RMAT) designation to AAV-GAD, its Parkinson’s disease treatment.
  • The RMAT designation is usually granted for cellular, genetic or other biologic therapies and entails benefits such as early interactions with the FDA to discuss surrogate or intermediate endpoints that could support the accelerated approval and meet post-approval requirements.
  • This status was awarded after positive data from 3 clinical studies, the company said.

data.title: Kraft Heinz asks suppliers for 60-days' notice for coffee price hikes - Reuters
data.description:
Kraft Heinz (NASDAQ:KHC) is asking its coffee suppliers to provide the company with 60-days’ notice before raising prices, according to an internal document viewed by Reuters.

The parent company of Maxwell House, McCafé, Gevalia, and Yuban buys most of its raw coffee beans sourced from tropical regions including Brazil, Vietnam, Colombia, and Indonesia, all of which are subject to U.S. trade tariffs of at least 10%. And as contracts between the grower and buyer stipulate the buyer is responsible for import fees at the market of destination, Kraft Heinz could be forced to absorb the higher costs imposed on its coffee suppliers. 

According to the document cited by Reuters sent in April to its suppliers, Kraft Heinz (NASDAQ:KHC) asked for prices to be raised only if tariffs are permanent and to immediately reverse them if they are removed.

Faced with a price sensitive consumer, Kraft (NASDAQ:KHC) must tread lightly when considering higher prices for a beverage enjoyed by 66% of Americans. As a result of severe droughts in Brazil and massive flooding in Vietnam (both of which account for 56% of global coffee supply), the price of a pound of coffee is expected to increase by at least 20% this year—excluding any impact on import tariffs. This puts the cost of a cup of home-brewed coffee at $0.22, up from $0.18 per cup at the beginning of the year.

And while coffee represents just 3% of Kraft Heinz’s (NASDAQ:KHC) total business, it brought in $835M in revenue in 2024, according to the company's latest annual report.

data.title: Celsius ex-CEO Alex Mashinsky sentenced to 12 years for crypto fraud
data.description:
Alex Mashinsky, the founder of collapsed crypto lending platform Celsius Network, was sentenced to 12 years in prison Thursday for defrauding investors and manipulating the company's native token CEL (CEL-USD).

That's a smaller prison term than the 20 years the U.S. Justice Department wanted the court to impose.

Judge John Koeltl in the Southern District of New York delivered the sentence, describing Mashinsky's crimes as "extremely serious" and rejecting the defense's request for leniency. In addition to the prison term, Mashinsky was sentenced to three years of supervised release and ordered to pay a $50K fine and forfeiture of ~$48.4M. 

Marketed by Mashinsky as a safe, high-yield alternative to traditional banking products, Celsius managed over $20B in customer crypto assets at its 2021 peak. But, last December, Mashinsky pled guilty to securities and commodities fraud charges in a multi-billion dollar scheme.

"Alexander Mashinsky targeted retail investors with promises that he would keep their 'digital assets' safer than a bank, when in fact he used those assets to place risky bets and to line his own pockets," U.S. Attorney Jay Clayton said in a statement.

"In the end, Mashinsky made tens of millions of dollars while his customers lost billions. America’s investors deserve better. The case for tokenization and the use of digital assets is strong but it is not a license to deceive. The rules against fraud still apply, and the SDNY will hold those who flout them accountable for their crimes,” he added.

The sentencing marks the latest in a growing roaster of fallen crypto moguls, joining the likes FTX's Sam Bankman-Fried, Binance's Changpeng Zhao and Terraform's Do Kwon, all brought down by fraud convictions.

data.title: Franco-Nevada outlines 2025 record earnings and strategic growth drivers
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Franco-Nevada Corporation (FNV) Q1 2025

Management View

  • CEO Paul Brink highlighted Franco-Nevada's record financial results for Q1 2025, achieving its best-ever top and bottom-line performance despite no contributions from Cobre Panama. This achievement was supported by elevated gold prices, strong Hemlo NPI contributions, and robust energy production. Key growth drivers include Tocantinzinho, Greenstone, and Valentine Gold ramp-ups, alongside the new Porcupine royalty.
  • The acquisition of a $500 million stream on Sibanye-Stillwater's Western Limb Mining Operations and a $300 million royalty on Discovery Silver's Porcupine Complex were emphasized. The company remains debt-free with $2.1 billion in available capital.
  • CFO Sandip Rana reported a 38% year-over-year increase in average gold prices and a 37% increase in silver prices for Q1 2025. Total GEOs sold reached 126,585, reflecting a 3% increase. Revenues from diversified assets also grew by 21% year-over-year despite higher gold prices impacting GEO conversions.

Outlook

  • Management maintained a positive outlook, driven by ongoing ramp-ups in Tocantinzinho, Greenstone, and Valentine Gold. Additional contributions are anticipated from the Porcupine Complex royalty.
  • The company highlighted the significant impact of high-margin royalty GEOs on future growth, noting a shift in focus from streams to royalties.
  • Forecasts for 2025 include total GEO sales between 465,000 and 525,000, with precious metals contributing 385,000 to 425,000 GEOs.

Financial Results

  • Total revenue for Q1 2025 was $368.4 million, a 43% increase from Q1 2024, driven by higher gold and silver prices and additional contributions from recent acquisitions.
  • Adjusted EBITDA for the quarter reached $321.9 million, a 49% increase year-over-year. Adjusted net income was $205.6 million or $1.07 per share, reflecting a 51% increase.
  • Net GEOs sold rose by 6% year-over-year to 113,138, with cash costs per GEO reported at $304, resulting in a margin exceeding $2,500 per GEO.

Q&A

  • Heiko Ihle, H.C. Wainwright: Asked about trends in stream-to-royalty conversions. CEO Paul Brink clarified that there were no current thematic trends in such conversions and that these decisions are transaction-specific.
  • Cosmos Chiu, CIBC: Inquired about NPI contributions from Hemlo and Kirkland Lake. CFO Sandip Rana indicated that Hemlo's Q1 contributions included a $5 million catch-up from 2024. For Kirkland Lake, no immediate contributions were expected due to current mining locations.
  • Daniel Major, UBS: Asked about the timeline for Western Limb GEO deliveries. CFO Rana explained a one-quarter lag, with annual contributions estimated at roughly 20,000 GEOs.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed cautious optimism, focusing on guidance clarity and growth drivers. Concerns centered on Cobre Panama's status and diversified asset contributions.
  • Management demonstrated confidence in addressing analyst queries, particularly regarding asset ramp-ups and financial stability. Phrases like "we are delighted" and "we remain well-capitalized" emphasized positivity.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management maintained a consistent tone, while analysts showed greater interest in new acquisitions and operational updates.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Revenue increased by $47.4 million compared to Q4 2024, supported by higher gold prices and new asset contributions.
  • Analysts' focus shifted from broader portfolio diversification to specific asset growth and operational execution.
  • Management tone remained consistent, emphasizing financial strength and strategic acquisitions.

Risks and Concerns

  • Cobre Panama's unresolved status remains a key uncertainty. Negotiations with the Panamanian government are ongoing, with arbitration proceedings as a fallback.
  • The impact of fluctuating commodity prices on GEO conversions was noted as a structural risk.
  • Analysts raised questions about the sustainability of NPI contributions and the ramp-up timelines of newly acquired assets.

Final Takeaway

Franco-Nevada demonstrated robust growth in Q1 2025, achieving record financial results driven by elevated gold prices and strategic acquisitions. Key growth drivers include the ramp-up of Tocantinzinho and Greenstone, alongside contributions from the Porcupine Complex royalty. The company remains well-capitalized to pursue additional opportunities while maintaining financial discipline and focusing on high-margin royalty assets. Analysts' discussions highlighted confidence in the company's strategic direction but also noted concerns about Cobre Panama and asset ramp-up timelines.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: BofA’s Hartnett: Investors prepare for ‘buy the expectation, sell the fact’
data.description:
Bank of America’s 2025 outlook signals a major pivot in investment strategies.

Michael Hartnett, investment strategist at Bank of America, said the bank has shifted from the 2020’s view of “anything but bonds” and “anywhere but China” towards preferences for bonds (SPLB), (BND), (BSV) over equities (SP500), (DJI), (COMP:IND), international stocks (ACWX) over U.S., and gold (NYSEARCA:GLD) over U.S. dollar (DXY).

This strategic realignment stems from anticipated clashes between U.S. exceptionalism and emerging populist policies that could impact markets.

The pace of U.S. nominal GDP growth, which surged 50% over the past five years, is expected to slow significantly, Hartnett warned.

The 2025 policy landscape appears increasingly restrictive as the Federal Reserve is projected to halt rate cuts while government spending could stagnate or decline by $50B.

Though the effective U.S. tariff rate may decrease overall, the strategist highlighted that potential tariffs on China (30%) and the EU (10%) would still represent “equivalent to >$600B rise in tariff taxation.”

These fiscal constraints could substantially reshape market dynamics through 2025.

Hartnett suggested that currently oversold equities have “correctly front-run Q2 trade deals/lower tariffs,” but cautions investors to prepare for a “buy the expectation, sell the fact” scenario when trade agreements materialize.

The strategist recommends maintaining short positions on the U.S. dollar (DXY) until the Fed resumes rate cuts and holding 5-year U.S. Treasuries (US5Y) until potential GOP tax cuts become formalized.

He said that macro catalysts that could fuel further market gains include the “3Cs,” a China deal, rate cuts (with China doing the biggest easing), and a strong consumer.

The primary risks to markets stem from potential “asset prices” collapse and subsequent deleveraging contagion, he added. This concern is amplified by recent data showing global debt reaching an all-time high $324T in the first quarter, a vulnerability that could trigger widespread market disruption if asset values decline precipitously.

data.title: New York Fed plans to offer morning repo operations soon
data.description:
After testing the use of a Standing Repo Facility (SRF) in a morning auction, the Federal Reserve Bank of New York plans to make early-settlement SRF auctions part of its regular daily schedule in addition to the existing afternoon settlements "in the not-too-distant future," it said on Friday.

In 2021, the New York Fed started SRFs to serve as a backstop in money markets. It essentially offers very short-term loans (overnight) to financial institutions, providing the cash to ensure smooth operations and liquid markets. The Fed buys a security from an eligible counterparty and agrees to sell it back the next day. The difference between the purchase prices and the sale price implies a rate of interest earned by the Fed on the transaction.

"These early-settlement auctions, combined with the current afternoon auctions, will enhance the effectiveness of the SRF as a tool for monetary policy implementation and market functioning," said Roberto Perli, the New York Fed's manager of the System Open Market Account.

The existing SRFs typically take place every business day from 1:30 PM to 1:45 PM ET.

The SOMA desk conducted an early-settlement SRF small-value exercise for the first time on March 5 to test capabilities. After a positive assessment of that exercise, it then conducted early-settlement SRF operations each day from March 27 through April 2, in addition to the regular afternoon operations.

"The operations went smoothly and were regarded as successful," Perli said.

"Our market outreach following the March quarter-end revealed that primary dealers see the early-settlement SRF operations as an enhancement that increases the likelihood that the SRF will be used when economically convenient to do so," he added.

And while the early settlements will enhance the use of SRF, there's still room for improvement, he said. "Where possible, Federal Reserve staff will continue to look for ways to improve the efficacy of the SRF."
